---
title: "Get started"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
bibliography: "references.bib"
chunk_output_type: "console"
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Get started}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

```{r}
library(stancer)
```

<!-- WARNING - This vignette is generated by {fusen} from dev/flat_stancer.Rmd: do not edit by hand -->




# Introduction

## Overview

**stancer** provides tools for automated stance analysis in R. It uses an ensemble of Large Language Models (LLM) to determine whether a text is in favour of, against, or neutral towards a specific target.

The package is built upon the **COLA** (Collaborative rOle-infused LLM-based Agents) framework proposed by @lan2024stancedetectioncollaborativeroleinfused. Instead of a single prompt, `stancer` coordinates a team of LLM agents—linguists, domain experts, and social media specialists—who analyse the text and debate its meaning before reaching a final judgement.

## Quick Example

```r
library(stancer)
library(ellmer)

# Set up your LLM client
chat <- chat_anthropic()

text <- "The carbon tax is just another way for the government to control our lives and stifle economic growth."
target <- "Climate Change mitigation policies"

result <- llm_stance(
  text,
  target,
  type = "object", # stance towards a given object or entity
  # type = "statement", # whether a text agrees with a certain statement
  chat_base = chat,
  domain_role = "economic analyst"
)

# View the summary
summary(result)
as.data.frame(result)
inspect(result, "analysis", "linguistic")
```

---

# Utility Functions

## Text Truncation


## Error Handling


## Expression Catching






## Input Validation








## Argument Recycling


## Timing


---

# Translations and Localization

## Load Translations


## Available Languages

```{r examples-stancer_available_languages}
stancer_available_languages()
```



## Localization Helper


## Check Translations

```{r examples-stancer_check_translations}
stancer_check_translations()
```



## Type to Term Conversion


---

# Scale System

## Scale Definitions


## Data Schema


---

# Prompt and Task Preparation

## Prompts



```{r examples-prompts-prepare}
# Prepare prompts for linguistic analysis
# templates <- list(
#   `system-linguist` = "prompts/en/system-linguist.md",
#   `user-linguist` = "prompts/en/user-linguist.md"
# )
# 
# prompts <- stancer:::prompts_prepare(
#   role = "linguist",
#   templates = templates,
#   language = "en",
#   target = "climate change",
#   text = c("some text", "and more"),
#   target_type = "the object"
# )
# 
# # Prepare prompts for domain expert
# templates <- list(
#   `system-domain` = "prompts/en/system-domain.md",
#   `user-domain` = "prompts/en/user-domain.md"
# )
# 
# prompts <- stancer:::prompts_prepare(
#   role = "domain",
#   templates = templates,
#   language = "en",
#   target = "artificial intelligence",
#   text = "AI will replace all human jobs",
#   target_type = "the object",
#   domain = "technology and employment expert"
# )
# 
# # Prepare prompts for judger with additional options
# templates <- list(
#   `system-judger` = "prompts/en/system-judger.md",
#   `user-judger` = "prompts/en/user-judger.md",
#   reference_resolution = "prompts/en/reference-resolution.md",
#   statement_resolution = "prompts/en/statement-resolution.md",
#   scale_guide = "prompts/en/guide-likert.md"
# )
# 
# prompts <- stancer:::prompts_prepare(
#   role = "judger",
#   templates = templates,
#   language = "en",
#   add_reference_resolution = TRUE,
#   add_statement_resolution = TRUE,
#   target = "Climate change is a real concern",
#   text = "Global warming is a serious threat",
#   target_type = "the statement",
#   FavourResponse = "This text is almost a paraphrase of the target statement",
#   AgainstResponse = "This is most probably a parody of climate speeches.",
#   NeutralResponse = "Context is missing and we should be sceptical"
# )
```



## Tasks




---

# Expert Analysis

## Prepare Expert Chats


## Execute Role


---

# Debate Stage

## Prepare Debater Chats


---

# judgement Stage

## Prepare Judger Chats


---

# Pipeline Stages

## Stage 1: Parallel Analysis


## Stage 2: Parallel Debates


## Stage 3: Parallel Judgement


---

# Main Analysis Function

## LLM Stance Detection

### GENERIC FUNCTION

### CHARACTER METHOD

```{r examples-llm_stance.character}
# result <- llm_stance(
#   text = "I support renewable energy",
#   target = "renewable energy",
#   chat_base = chat_model,
#   type = "object",
#   scale = "categorical"
# )
```



### DATA.FRAME METHOD

```{r examples-llm_stance.data.frame}
# results <- llm_stance(
#   data = tweets_df,
#   text = "text",
#   target = "climate_change",
#   chat_base = chat_model
# )
```



---

# Result Inspection and Display

## Print Method

```{r examples-print.stance_result}
# result <- llm_stance(
#   text = "R is smart and tidy",
#   target = "R programming language",
#   chat_base = ellmer::chat_anthropic()
# )
# print(result)
```



## Summary Method


## Data Frame Conversion


## Inspect Method


```{r examples-inspect.stance_result}
# result <- llm_stance(...)
# inspect(result, what = 'metadata')
# inspect(result, what = 'analysis', within = 'linguistic')
# inspect(result, what = 'debates', within = 'negative', index = 1)
```



---

# Inflate
